title: Chocolate blocks – it ought to be banned
excerpt: Around the big holidays we always go looking for a little sin in the pots. We hang on to all the excess. Now when the kids or the husband ask for a little something sweet – I make these blocks. I just make them in the microwave. It's rich, luscious and lekker. Small bites …

seo.title: Chocolate blocks - it ought to be banned - Marinda Kook

html:
Around the big holidays we always go looking for a little sin in the pots. We hang on to all the excess. Now when the kids or the husband ask for a little something sweet – I make these blocks. I just make them in the microwave. It's rich, luscious and lekker. Small bites!

 

YOU NEED:

  • 125ml castor sugar
  • 125 ml golden syrup
  • 125 ml peanut butter
  • 15 ml cocoa
  • 750 ml chocolate rice cereal (Coco Pops)
  • 180 g chocolate
  • 60 ml milk or cream

NOW:

  1. Put the castor sugar, golden syrup, peanut butter and cocoa in a bowl that can go into the microwave. Microwave on full power for 90 seconds – stir it. Another 60 seconds and then take it out. Stir it through properly.
  2. Add the rice cereal to the mixture and mix until all the rice cereal is coated with the sauce.
  3. Spray a dish with spray and cook and press the rice cereal mixture firmly into the dish.
  4. Break the chocolate into a bowl or cup and add the milk. Melt in the microwave for 1 minute on full power and stir until it's runny.
  5. Pour it over the rice cereal and put it in the fridge.
  6. After about an hour you can cut it into blocks and eat it.

It's a quick dessert, something to have with the coffee or something for when sleep escapes you in the night.

Remember, small bites.
